comment: Martin & Moyce (1952), 'Part IV: An experimental study of the collapse of liquid columns on a rigid horizontal plane', Phil. Trans. R. Soc. Lond. A 244, 312-324. Dimensionless surge-front position for the square-based water column (n^2 = 2 geometry, 2.25 in base). T = t*sqrt(2g/a); Z = x/a. Values are the DIGITIZED points commonly used across the LBM/SPH validation literature; digitization uncertainty is a few percent — treat as a report-only reference curve at coarse lattice resolution (the hard gate is the same monotone+envelope band the 2-D battery uses).
